pages.summary: This page introduces the document's purpose, target audience, accessibility features, organization structure, and related information.
This page introduces a user manual for Oracle FLEXCUBE's Management Information System module, detailing its purpose and target audience.
pages.raw_text:
1-1
1. Preface
1.1 
Introduction
This manual is designed to give you an understanding of the Management Information 
System module of Oracle FLEXCUBE. Apart from assisting you in generating reports, this 
manual guides you in the definition and maintenance of MIS classes and cost codes, MIS 
groups, MIS heads, MIS pools and refinancing rates. You are also taught how to define MIS 
details for a customer, an account class, a product and a contract.
This user manual is a supplement to the Common Core - Core Entities and Services user 
manual and contains only specific functionalities and information related to Oracle FCUBS 
Management Information System. Hence, this document should be read in conjunction with 
the Common Core - Core Entities and Services user manual from the perspective of 
completeness in flow and understanding. 
1.2 
Audience
This manual is intended for the Customer Service Representatives (CSRs) and the bank staff 
in charge of setting up new products in your bank.
1.3 
Documentation Accessibility
For information about Oracle's commitment to accessibility, visit the Oracle Accessibility 
Program website at http://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=acc&id=docacc.
1.4 
Organization
This manual is organized as follows:
Chapter
Description
Chapter 1
About this Manual gives information on the intended audience. It 
also lists the various chapters covered in this User Manual.
Chapter 2
Defining MIS Class Code describes how to define MIS classes and 
link them to several entities.
Chapter 3
MIS Cascading explains the details on MIS cascading. 
Chapter 4
Defining MIS Heads describes the procedure for defining MIS 
heads. This includes the linking of an MIS head to an accounting 
entry, indicating whether the MIS head should be considered for refi-
nancing and the operations that can be performed on an existing 
MIS head record. 
Chapter 5
Making MIS Adjustments for a Pool specifies the manner in which 
you can allocate the amounts to be reported from one MIS code to 
another. 
Chapter 6
Defining MIS details for a Customer, Account Class, Account, Prod-
uct, and Contract enumerates the steps that you should follow while 
defining MIS details for a customer, an account class, a product or a 
contract.

1-2
1.5 
Glossary of Icons

1.6 
Related Documents

Chapter 7

used in the module with page references for quick navigation.
Icons

sections: Maintaining Allowed List of MIS Codes
pages.summary: This page explains what an MIS Class is and how it is used for flexible reporting in a bank's centralized database architecture, along with details on maintaining restrictions related to MIS codes.
pages.raw_text:
2-1
2. Defining MIS Class Codes
In simple terms, an MIS Class represents a type of entity based on which you want your 
reporting to be done. An MIS Class will be the most general definition for MIS reporting, under 
which you can have several entities. 
For example, if you want reports on the profitability of accounts officers, you would define 
Accounts Officer as an MIS Class. Accounts Officers in your bank would report to this MIS 
Class. Similarly, you define Cost Center as an MIS Class if you want reports based on the 
cost centers in your bank. You can have the various cost centers in your bank linked to the 
MIS Class of Cost Center. Business Segment, Industry Segments are other examples of an 
MIS Class.
The definition of MIS Class renders the entire MIS reporting absolutely flexible. By defining a 
class of your choice, and linking several entities to it, you can generate management 
information as per your requirement.
In a centralized database architecture, an MIS Class is defined in the Head Office branch of 
your bank, and is used by all the branches on the database.
This chapter contains the following section:
пБђпА†Section 2.1, "MIS Code Restriction Maintenance"
2.1 
MIS Code Restriction Maintenance
You can maintain MIS Code restriction maintenance for MIS Class, Product or Account Class, 
and Branch.
This section contains the following topic:
пБђпА†Section 2.1.1, "Maintaining Allowed List of MIS Codes"
2.1.1 
Maintaining Allowed List of MIS Codes
You can maintain a list of allowed MIS codes for a given MIS class, product or account class 
combination for a branch in the вАШMIS Code Allowed For MIS Class and Product/Account ClassвАЩ 
screen.The MIS call forms would pick only the MIS codes that are allowed for the product or account 
class and MIS class combination. You can invoke the 'MIS Code Restriction Maintenance' screen 
by typing вАШMIDPRDMNвАЩ in the field at the top right corner of the Application tool bar and 
clicking on the adjoining arrow button.

pages.summary: This page describes how to specify and select various details such as branch code, MIS class, product or account class codes for maintaining allowed lists in a system.
pages.raw_text:
2-2
You can specify the following details:
Branch Code
Specify the branch code. Alternatively, you can select the branch code from the option list. 
The list displays all the branch code for which the MIS code restrictions are maintained.
Branch Name
The system displays the name of the branch maintained in the system.
MIS Class
Specify the MIS class for which the allowed list of MIS codes are maintained. Alternatively, you can 
select the class code from the option list. The list displays all the MIS classes maintained in 
the system. The customer MIS classes are not part of the list.
MIS Class Description
The system displays the MIS class description maintained in the system.
Product / Account Class Code
Specify the product code or account class code for which the allowed lists are maintained. 
Alternatively, you can select the code from the option list. The list displays all the products or 
account class code maintained in the system.
Product Or Account Class Description
The system displays the product or account class description respectively maintained in the 
system.

pages.summary: This page describes how to specify and manage MIS codes in a system, including viewing their descriptions, allowing them for specific combinations, and accessing the restriction summary screen.
pages.raw_text:
2-3
MIS Codes
MIS Code
Specify the MIS code maintained. Alternatively, you can select the MIS code from the option 
list. The list displays all the MIS codes maintained in the system.
MIS Code Description
The system displays the MIS code description maintained in the system.
Allowed
Check this box to allow the MIS code for a given MIS class, product or account class combination for a 
branch.
Note 
The MIS call forms attached to a transaction input or an account creation screen populates 
the MIS codes that are allowed for a given MIS class, product or account class combina-
tion for a branch. If you do not perform maintenance for a given branch, account class/
product code and MIS class combination, then all MIS Classes linked to the account class/
product code will be available. 
2.1.2 
Viewing MIS Code Restriction Summary
You can view the MIS code restriction summary in the вАШMIS Code Restriction SummaryвАЩ 
screen. It displays the status of the records maintained, whether it is authorized, unauthorized 
or rejected.
You can invoke this screen by typing вАШMISPRDMNвАЩ in the field at the top right corner of the 
Application tool bar and clicking on the adjoining arrow button.

pages.summary: This page describes how to use query parameters such as Authorization Status, Branch Code, MIS Class, Record Status, and Product/Account Class to search for records in a system.
pages.raw_text:
2-4
In the above screen, you can base your queries on any or all of the following parameters and 
fetch records:
пБђпА†Authorization Status
пБђпА†Branch Code
пБђпА†MIS Class
пБђпА†Record Status
пБђпА†Product/Account Class
Click вАШSearchвАЩ button. The system identifies all records satisfying the specified criteria and 
displays the following details for each one of them:
пБђпА†Authorization Status 
пБђпА†Record Status 
пБђпА†Branch Code 
пБђпА†Product/Account Class 
пБђпА†MIS Class

pages.summary: This page explains how MIS cascading works and allows defaulting specific MIS groups in various screens during record creation, with a batch program updating these values at EOD. It also describes the maintenance of feature IDs for branches across multiple screens.
pages.raw_text:
3-1
3. MIS Cascading
This chapter contains the following sections:
пБђпА†Section 3.1, "MIS Cascading "
пБђпА†Section 3.2, "Feature ID Maintenance "
3.1 
MIS Cascading 
You can have a particular MIS group defaulted in the MIS Subsystem of the following screens 
at the time of creation of records:
пБђпА†Customer Maintenance Screen
пБђпА†Account Class Maintenance Screen
пБђпА†Customer Account Maintenance Screen
пБђпА†All the Products Screens
пБђпА†All the Contract Screens
Once these records are authorized, you can change the MIS Group values (i.e. MIS Codes 
linked to the MIS Class) in the MIS Group Maintenance screen. During EOD, the batch 
program вАШMIGCASCDвАЩ maintained at the Post EOTI level will cascade the modified MIS 
Codes in the above screens. As a result, the system replaces the MIS Codes that are provided 
at the time of creation of the above records by the MIS Codes maintained in MIS Group.
3.2 
Feature ID Maintenance 
If you maintain feature ID for all branches, the system displays the MIS class values and MIS 
codes in the MIS subsystem of the following screens.
пБђпА†Customer Maintenance Screen
пБђпА†Account Class Maintenance Screen
пБђпА†Customer Account Maintenance Screen
пБђпА†All the Products Screens
пБђпА†All the Contract Screens

pages.summary: This page explains how to maintain and link accounting entries to MIS Heads for profitability reporting purposes.
pages.raw_text:
4-1
4. Defining MIS Heads
Each accounting entry that is generated can be linked to an MIS Head. An MIS Head indicates 
the manner in which the type of entry should be considered for profitability reporting purposes.
This chapter contains the following section:
пБђпА†Section 4.1, "MIS Head Maintenance"
4.1 
MIS Head Maintenance
This section contains the following topics:
пБђпА†Section 4.1.1, "Invoking the MIS Heads Screen"
пБђпА†Section 4.1.2, "Linking an MIS Head to an Accounting Entry"
пБђпА†Section 4.1.3, "Operations on the MIS Head Record"
4.1.1 
Invoking the MIS Heads Screen
You can invoke the вАШMIS Head MaintenanceвАЩ screen by typing вАШMIDHEADвАЩ in the field at the 
top right corner of the Application toolbar and clicking the adjoining arrow button.
If you are creating a new MIS Head, select вАШNewвАЩ from the Actions Menu in the Application 
toolbar or click new icon. The вАШMIS HeadвАЩ screen is displayed without any details.
If you are calling an MIS Head that has already been defined, double-click an MIS Head from 
the summary screen.
Category
The MIS Head Category classification indicates the method in which the accounting entry will 
be reported in the profitability report. It could be one of the following:

pages.summary: This page explains different types of MIS Heads used in accounting entries for balance, income, expense, and contingent entries, along with their classification purposes and an example of the 'Refinance' option.
pages.raw_text:
4-2
Balance
Indicates that the accounting entry should be considered for calculating the Cumulative 
Average Balance. Typically, an asset entry would be classified under the balance type MIS 
Head. The GL entry leg of a loan disbursement transaction, would typically be classified under 
a Balance type of MIS Head.
Income
Indicates that the accounting entry should be considered as an income for the purpose of 
profitability reporting. Clearly, any interest or charge income would be classified under an 
Income type of MIS Head.
Expense
Indicates that the accounting entry should be considered as an expense for the purpose of 
profitability reporting. Any interest, postage, or tax expense would be classified under an 
expense type of MIS Head.
Contingent
Indicates that the accounting entry should be considered as a contingent entry for the purpose 
of profitability reporting. A forward FX deal, the issue of an import Letter of Credit, etc., are 
examples of contingent type of entries. You can generate a report that gives the balances due 
to such contingent type of entries.
Under each type of MIS Head, you may define additional MIS Heads, as per your reporting 
requirement. For example, you can have an MIS Head for each loan product, so that you can 
have the cumulative average balance due to all the loans disbursed under the product. 
Refinance
The вАШRefinanceвАЩ option is provided for marking MIS Heads for refinancing. For MIS Heads that 
are marked for refinancing, the refinancing income or expense would be calculated, on the 
cumulative average balance, for the reporting period. You would typically mark a balance type 
of MIS Head for refinancing.
This option is provided because not all the balance type of entries would come under the 
umbrella of refinancing. To enable this option select the check box beside вАШRefinanceвАЩ.
4.1.2 
Linking an MIS Head to an Accounting Entry
Each accounting entry generated in Oracle FLEXCUBE will have a transaction code that 
describes the nature of the entry. These transaction codes are defined by you, depending on 
your requirements. 
An MIS Head can be attached to a transaction code. A transaction code is attached to an 
accounting entry when an event is defined for a product, through the вАШProduct Event 
Accounting Entries MaintenanceвАЩ screen. Thus, whenever a transaction with an MIS Head 
attached is entered for an entry, the MIS Head will automatically be linked to it. You can 
change this while defining the product.

sections: 4.1.3 Operations on the MIS Head Record
pages.summary: This page describes how to link MIS Heads to accounting entries and provides details on operations that can be performed on an existing MIS Head record.
pages.raw_text:
4-3
Alternatively, you can link an MIS Head to an accounting entry directly while defining the 
product. 
You should link the appropriate leg of the accounting entry to the MIS Head to which it 
belongs. Ensure that you link only one leg of the entry to an MIS Head. 
To add a record to the list of вАШAccounting EntriesвАЩ click add icon. Choose values for each field 
of a record using the adjoining option list.
4.1.3 
Operations on the MIS Head Record
On an existing MIS Head record, you can perform the following operations (if any function 
under the Actions Menu is disabled, it means that the function is not allowed for the record):
пБђпА†Amend the details of an MIS Head
пБђпА†Authorize an MIS Head
пБђпА†Copy the details an MIS Head on to a new one
пБђпА†Print the details of an MIS Head
пБђпА†Delete an MIS Head
Please refer to the manual on common procedures for details of these operations.

sections: Operations on the MIS Adjustments Screen
pages.summary: This page explains how to make adjustments in Management Information System (MIS) codes for a pool, specifically focusing on maintaining and using the MIS Adjustments screen.
pages.raw_text:
5-1
5. Making MIS adjustments for a Pool
Within a pool, you may sometimes want to allocate the amounts to be reported from one MIS 
Code to another.
For instance, the Financial Control (FINCON) department in your bank is the one that 
disburses the salaries for your staff. This department is defined as an MIS Code under the 
MIS Class Cost Center. 
Now, there are other cost centers, such as: loans department, time deposits department, 
trade financing department and retail banking department. 
When the salary is disbursed by the FINCON department every month, a debit entry shown 
for the MIS Code FINCON. The burden of this debit entry should be shared appropriately by 
the various departments to which the staff of your bank belong.
This distribution of the salary amount is done through the MIS adjustments screen.
Thus, MIS adjustments can be done between two MIS Codes, which belong to the same pool, 
and MIS Class. 
This chapter contains the following section:
пБђпА†Section 5.1, "MIS Adjustment Maintenance"
5.1 
MIS Adjustment Maintenance
This section contains the following topics:
пБђпА†Section 5.1.1, "Invoking the MIS Adjustments Screen"
пБђпА†Section 5.1.2, "Operations on the MIS Adjustments Screen"

sections: 5.1.2 Operations on the MIS Adjustments Screen
pages.summary: This page explains how to invoke and use the 'MIS Adjustment Maintenance' screen for inputting adjustment details, including necessary fields and operations.
pages.raw_text:
5-2
5.1.1 
Invoking the MIS Adjustments Screen
You can invoke the вАШMIS Adjustment MaintenanceвАЩ screen by typing вАШMIDADJвАЩ in the field at 
the top right corner of the Application tool bar and clicking on the adjoining arrow button.
Select вАШNewвАЩ from the Actions Menu in the Application toolbar or click new icon to input the 
details of an adjustment. The вАШMIS AdjustmentsвАЩ screen is displayed without any details. You 
can input the following details of the adjustment.
пБђпА†Reference Number
пБђпА†Pool Code
пБђпА†MIS Class
пБђпА†MIS Head
пБђпА†Currency
Adjustment Details and Time Details
You have to specify the following details while doing an adjustment:
пБђпА†The pool code, MIS Class and MIS Head under which you want to make the adjustment.
пБђпА†The currency in which the adjustment amount is represented.
пБђпА†The MIS Code from which the amount should be moved (the debit MIS Code).
пБђпА†The MIS Code to which the amount should be moved (the credit MIS Code).
пБђпА†The amount that has to be moved or adjusted.
пБђпА†The period for which the adjustment should be considered (represented through the 
month and the year under the вАШTime DetailsвАЩ).
Depending on the type of MIS Head - balance, income, expense, or contingent, the adjusted 
amount will be reported accordingly, for the appropriate period.
5.1.2 
Operations on the MIS Adjustments Screen
Once you input the details of an adjustment, it should be authorized by a user bearing a 
different Login ID from yours.
Please refer to the manual on common procedures for details of these operations.

sections: MIS Details for a Customer, Account Class and Product
Defining MIS Details for a Customer
pages.summary: This section explains how to define MIS details for customers, including their MIS group, and provides instructions on using the 'MIS' screen during customer code definition.
pages.raw_text:
6-1
6. Defining MIS Details for a Customer, Account
Class, Account, Product, and Contract
This chapter contains the following sections:
пБђпА†Section 6.1, "MIS Details for a Customer, Account Class and Product"
пБђпА†Section 6.2, "MIS Details for a Contract and Account"
пБђпА†Section 6.3, "GL MIS Balance Transfers due to Reclassification"
6.1 
MIS Details for a Customer, Account Class and Prod-
uct
This section contains the following topics:
пБђпА†Section 6.1.1, "Defining MIS Details for a Customer"
пБђпА†Section 6.1.2, "Change Log Button"
пБђпА†Section 6.1.3, "Defining MIS Details Screen for an Account Class"
пБђпА†Section 6.1.4, "Defining MIS Details Screen for a Product"
пБђпА†Section 6.1.5, "Specifying the MIS Details for an Account Class and a Product"
6.1.1 
Defining MIS Details for a Customer
The MIS details for a customer can be defined through the вАШMISвАЩ screen. This screen can be 
invoked by clicking on the вАШMISвАЩ button while defining the customer code. The вАШMISвАЩ details 
screen for a customer is shown below.
While defining a customer, you can define the following MIS details:
MIS Group
The MIS Group to which the customer should be linked is defined in the вАШMIS GroupвАЩ field (if 
it is applicable, if not, you can define the individual entities under which the customer should 
be reviewed). If a group is linked, the entities defined for the group will be displayed, and these 
entities can be changed. Only the Customer and Composite type of MIS Classes will be

sections: Customer MIS Code and Composite MIS Code
pages.summary: This page discusses how MIS codes are managed for customers, including linking groups, defaulting codes, and maintaining change logs.
pages.raw_text:
6-2
applicable for customers. The specific MIS entity (in the form of an MIS Code) will be picked 
up by default only if you have defined them for the group. If not, you have to indicate the 
appropriate MIS code for each applicable MIS class.
Link to Group
If an MIS Group is linked, you can indicate whether the linkage with the group should always 
be maintained. If yes, any change to the MIS Group will automatically apply to the customer 
to whom the MIS Group is linked. If not, the entities defaulted for the customer will continue, 
even if they are changed subsequently for the group.
Customer MIS Code and Composite MIS Code
The details defined for a customer will be defaulted, whenever a contract (a loan, deposit, 
letter of credit, bill, etc.) is processed for the customer. The details of the customer and 
composite class of entities will be picked up by default. These entities are defaulted even 
when you are opening an account for the customer. 
6.1.2 
Change Log Button
If MIS reclassification occurs, through changing the MIS codes for the вАШCustomer TypeвАЩ of MIS 
classes, the system stores the changes made, in a Customer MIS вАШChange LogвАЩ screen.
Click вАШChange LogвАЩ button in the Customer MIS screen to view the Customer MIS Change log.
The following details are stored in the Customer MIS Change Log for the customer in respect 
of which the MIS reclassification occurred:
пБђпА†Customer Number
пБђпА†MIS Class that was changed
пБђпА†Date on which the change was made
пБђпА†The old code for the changed MIS Class (this information is stored only for the first 
amendment during a day)
пБђпА†The new MIS code for the changed MIS class
6.1.3 
Defining MIS Details Screen for an Account Class
The MIS details for an account class and a product are defined in a similar manner. The 
procedures involved in defining the MIS details for an account class and a product are 
described below.

sections: 6.1.4 Defining MIS Details Screen for a Product
6.1.5 Specifying the MIS Details for an Account Class and a Product
pages.summary: This page explains how to invoke and define the 'MIS Details' screen for account classes and products, including specifying the MIS Group and MIS Code.
pages.raw_text:
6-3
The MIS details screen for an account class can be invoked by clicking on the MIS button 
while defining the account class.
6.1.4 
Defining MIS Details Screen for a Product
The вАШMIS DetailsвАЩ screen for a product can be invoked by clicking on the вАШMISвАЩ button while 
defining the product. 
6.1.5 
Specifying the MIS Details for an Account Class and a Product
The MIS details you would define for an account class and a product are similar. You should 
specify the following:
MIS Group
The MIS Group to which the account class or product should be linked is specified in the вАШMIS 
GroupвАЩ field (if it is applicable; if not, you can define the individual entities under which the 
product or account class should be reviewed). If a group is linked, the entities defined for the 
group will be displayed and these entities can be changed. Only the transaction type of MIS 
Class will be applicable for an account class or product. The specific MIS entity (in the form 
of an MIS Code) will be picked up by default only if you have defined them for the group. If 
not, you have to indicate the appropriate MIS code for each applicable MIS classes.

sections: MIS Details for a Contract and Account
pages.summary: This page discusses how MIS (Management Information System) group links affect account classes or products, default settings from the MIS group, pool codes, and details defined for contracts and accounts.
pages.raw_text:
6-4
Link to Group
If an MIS Group is linked, you can indicate whether the linkage with the group should always 
be maintained. If yes, any change done to the MIS Group will automatically be applied to the 
account class or product to which the MIS Group is linked. If not, the entities defaulted for the 
account class or product will be continued even if they are changed subsequently for the 
group.
Default from MIS Group
The details defined for an account class will default to account opened under the class. The 
details defined for the product will be picked up for the contracts processed under it.
Pool Code
The Pool Code to which the account class or product belongs should be defined.
6.2 
MIS Details for a Contract and Account
The MIS details you would define for an account class and a product are similar. The entities 
defined for the customer will be picked up by default and these entities can be changed.
This section contains the following topics:
пБђпА†Section 6.2.1, "Defining the MIS Details Screen for a Contract"
пБђпА†Section 6.2.2, "Change Log Button"
пБђпА†Section 6.2.3, "Defining the MIS Details Screen for an Account"
пБђпА†Section 6.2.4, "Change Log Button"
6.2.1 
Defining the MIS Details Screen for a Contract
To invoke the MIS details screen for a contract, click on the вАШMISвАЩ button while processing the 
contract.
For a contract, the transaction type of MIS class, the cost code and pool code will be picked 
up from the product under which the contract is processed. The composite MIS code will be 
picked up from the definition made for the customer, on behalf of whom the contract is being 
processed.

pages.summary: This section describes how changes in MIS codes are logged when reclassification occurs and details what information is stored in the Contract MIS Change Log.
pages.raw_text:
6-5
The interest calculation method for the refinancing rates of the pool will also be picked up, by 
default, from Pool Codes maintenance and these can be changed. For an account, the 
transaction type of MIS class will be picked up from the account class, along with the cost 
codes and pool codes. The composite type of MIS class will be defaulted from those defined 
for the customer. These too can be changed.
6.2.2 
Change Log Button
If MIS reclassification occurs, through changing the MIS codes for transaction or composite 
classes, the System stores the changes made, in a Contract MIS Change Log. The following 
details are stored in the Contract MIS Change Log for the contract in respect of which the MIS 
reclassification occurred:
пБђпА†Contract Reference Number
пБђпА†MIS Class that was changed
пБђпА†Date on which the change was made. 
пБђпА†The old code for the changed MIS Class (this information is stored only for the first 
amendment during a day)
пБђпА†The new MIS code for the changed MIS class.
Click вАШChange LogвАЩ button in the вАШTransaction MIS MaintenanceвАЩ screen to view the Contract 
MIS Change Log.

sections: Defining the MIS Details Screen for an Account
pages.summary: This page explains how to access the Management Information System screen and describes the details stored in the Account MIS Change Log when there are changes to the MIS codes.
pages.raw_text:
6-6
6.2.3 
Defining the MIS Details Screen for an Account
To invoke the вАШManagement Information SystemвАЩ screen for an account, click on the вАШMISвАЩ 
button while opening or modifying the account. 
6.2.4 
Change Log Button
If MIS reclassification occurs, through changing the MIS codes for the transaction or 
composite type of MIS classes for the customer account, the system stores the changes 
made, in an Account MIS Change Log. The following details are stored in the Account MIS 
Change Log for the customer account in respect of which the MIS reclassification occurred:
пБђпА†Account Number
пБђпА†Account Branch
пБђпА†MIS Class that was changed
пБђпА†Date on which the change was made. 
пБђпА†The old code for the changed MIS Class (this information is stored only for the first 
amendment during a day)
пБђпА†The new MIS code for the changed MIS class.

sections: GL MIS Balance Transfers due to Reclassification
pages.summary: This section explains how balances are transferred between GL accounts during reclassification and details what information is stored in the MIS Balance Transfer Log.
pages.raw_text:
6-7
Click вАШChange LogвАЩ button in the вАШAccount MISвАЩ screen to view the Account MIS Change log.
6.3 
GL MIS Balance Transfers due to Reclassification
This section contains the following topic:
пБђпА†Section 6.3.1, "Viewing Balance Transfer Log"
6.3.1 
Viewing Balance Transfer Log
When an MIS reclassification occurs in respect of a customer, account or contract, the 
balances in a GL associated with the old MIS code in each case are transferred to the GL for 
the new MIS code, if the option of transferring MIS balances upon reclassification has been 
set in the Chart of Accounts for the GL.
A batch process executes a GL MIS balance transfer upon MIS reclassification, for those GLs 
for which the balance transfer option has been set. The transfer is performed taking into 
account the details stored in the change logs (Customer MIS Change Log, Account MIS 
Change Log or Contract MIS Change Log). When the transfer is performed, the transfer 
details are stored in the MIS Balance Transfer Log. The following details relating to the 
transfer are stored:
пБђпА†Branch
пБђпА†GL
пБђпА†Period Code
пБђпА†Financial Cycle
пБђпА†Currency
пБђпА†Reference Type
пБђпА†Reference Number (Account, Contract, Customer Number)
пБђпА†MIS Class
пБђпА†Old MIS Code
пБђпА†New MIS Code
пБђпА†Transfer Indicator (Dr or Cr into New MIS Code).
пБђпА†Amount
пБђпА†Transaction Date

sections: You can view the MIS вАШBalance Transfer LogвАЩ for a customer, customer account or contract
The GL MIS Balance Transfer Process
pages.summary: This page explains how to access the Balance Transfer Log in different maintenance screens and provides an example of the GL MIS balance transfer process.
pages.raw_text:
6-8
You can view the MIS вАШBalance Transfer LogвАЩ for a customer, customer account or contract 
by clicking on вАШTransfer LogвАЩ button in the Customer MIS, Customer Account MIS, or 
Transaction MIS Maintenance screens.
The GL MIS Balance Transfer Process
The following example explains how the batch process executes the transfer of GL MIS 
balances.
